title: phaius gracilis
intro: Phaius gracilis, also known as the grass-pitcher orchid, is a type of terrestrial orchid native to the tropical regions of Asia. Traditionally used in folk medicine, Phaius gracilis has been found to possess medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, which have been used to treat various health conditions. The plant has been associated with several health benefits, including reducing fever, alleviating respiratory issues, and promoting wound healing. In herbal preparations, Phaius gracilis is often combined with other medicinal plants and used as a tea, decoction, or tincture to harness its medicinal properties.
